input.q:
Produce a supplier due-diligence risk profile on the packaging manufacturer "Crown Holdings, Inc." (the beverage and food can maker).

Cover, using public and reputable sources:
- Corporate identity and ownership: legal entity, HQ, stock listing/ticker, ultimate parent, key subsidiaries
- Financial health signals: latest reported revenue, profitability, debt or credit-rating commentary
- Legal and regulatory exposure: notable litigation, regulatory actions, fines or settlements in the last 5 years
- Sanctions or export-control exposure, if any
- Product recalls or quality issues
- ESG, environmental and labour controversies

Return a short executive risk summary (low/medium/high with reasoning), then the detailed findings by category with dates. Cite the source URL for every material claim and explicitly flag gaps where evidence was not found.

Sourcing and citation rules (apply to every claim):
- For each factual claim, cite the specific source that directly states that claim. Never attach a citation that does not itself contain the claim.
- Never cite a document dated before an event as the source for that event; the citation's own date must be consistent with the claim.
- Strongly prefer primary and authoritative sources: official company filings and press releases, regulators and government bodies, courts, standards bodies, and reputable news or trade press. Do not base a factual claim on SEO/marketing blogs, content farms, or open B2B marketplace listings; use such sources only if nothing better exists, and then explicitly label the claim as low-confidence.
- Label every figure as reported, estimate, or forecast, and give its date or as-of period.
- For any dramatic, contested, or fast-moving event, calibrate your language to what is actually confirmed, distinguish confirmed facts from uncertain ones, note where sources disagree on dates or numbers, and anchor to the most authoritative source available.
- If you cannot find a solid source for a claim, say so explicitly instead of asserting it.

output.answer:
## Executive Summary

**Risk Rating: Medium**
Crown Holdings, Inc. is a financially stable, publicly traded global packaging manufacturer with strong reported revenues ($12.36 billion in 2025) and stable credit ratings. However, the company presents a medium supplier risk profile due to a recent history of antitrust penalties (a €31.5 million EU cartel settlement in 2022), ongoing U.S. customs litigation involving up to $18.1 million in potential fines, a $1.9 million environmental penalty in Washington state (2021–2023), and significant legacy asbestos liabilities. Additionally, there is conflicting open-source reporting regarding the extent of the company's continued operational footprint in Russia.

---

## Detailed Findings

### Corporate Identity and Ownership
*   **Legal Entity and Headquarters:** The exact legal entity name is Crown Holdings, Inc., a Pennsylvania corporation [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm). The company's principal executive offices are located at Hidden River Corporate Center Two, 14025 Riveredge Drive, Suite 300, Tampa, FL 33637 [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm). *(Note: Older filings list Philadelphia, PA, but the most recent 2024 10-K specifies Tampa [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm).)*
*   **Stock Listing:** The company's common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol CCK [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm).
*   **Ultimate Parent and Subsidiaries:** Crown Holdings, Inc. is the ultimate parent company [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm). Key subsidiaries include Crown Americas LLC (PA), Crown Cork & Seal Company, Inc. (PA), Crown European Holdings S.A. (France), Crown Metal Packaging Canada LP, and Crown Americas Capital Corp. (DE) [Crown Holdings, Inc. - Form S-4](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/25890/000119312506027249/ds4.htm), [cck-20241231](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1219601/000121960125000009/cck-20241231.htm).

### Financial Health Signals
*   **Revenue and Profitability:** Crown Holdings reported net sales of $12,365 million for the full year 2025, an increase from $11,801 million in 2024 [CROWN HOLDINGS, INC. REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2025 RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/crown-holdings-inc-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results-302679438.html). The company reported a 2025 net income of $738 million and a record adjusted EBITDA of approximately $2.1 billion [Why Crown Holdings (CCK) Is Up 9.5% After Record 2025 EBITDA and Mixed Net Income Performance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/why-crown-holdings-cck-9-130654307.html), [Crown Holdings, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results | Crown](https://www.crowncork.com/news/crown-holdings-inc-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results). In Q1 2026, net sales were $3,259 million with a net income of $175 million [Crown Holdings Q1 2026 revenue and EPS update | CCK 8-K Filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CCK/8-k-crown-holdings-inc-reports-material-event-454ca6b99ff4.html).
*   **Debt Levels:** As of March 31, 2026, the company reported total debt of $5,964 million and cash of $764 million, resulting in a net debt of $5,200 million [Crown Holdings Q1 2026 revenue and EPS update | CCK 8-K Filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CCK/8-k-crown-holdings-inc-reports-material-event-454ca6b99ff4.html). *(Note: One secondary source reported Q1 2026 net debt at $5.67 billion, contradicting the company's 8-K filing [Crown Holdings, Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Hits $3.26 Billion](https://www.sci-tech-today.com/news/crown-holdings-inc-q1-2026-earnings/).)* The adjusted net leverage ratio improved to 2.5x as of March 31, 2026, down from 2.7x at the end of 2025 [Crown Holdings Q1 2026 revenue and EPS update | CCK 8-K Filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CCK/8-k-crown-holdings-inc-reports-material-event-454ca6b99ff4.html).
*   **Credit Ratings:** S&P Global Ratings affirmed Crown Holdings' Foreign Currency LT credit rating at 'BB+' with a stable outlook on June 13, 2025 [S&P Global Ratings affirms Crown Holdings at 'BB+' (Foreign Currency LT credit rating); outlook stable](https://cbonds.com/news/3447153/). Fitch Ratings has also previously affirmed the company at 'BB+' [Fitch Affirms Crown's Ratings at 'BB+'](https://www.fitchratings.com/research/corporate-finance/fitch-affirms-crown-ratings-at-bb-01-03-2013).

### Legal and Regulatory Exposure
*   **Antitrust Fines (EU):** On July 12, 2022, the European Commission fined Crown Holdings and Silgan a total of €31.5 million for participating in a metal packaging cartel in Germany between 2011 and 2014 [Violation Tracker - Good Jobs First](https://violationtracker.goodjobsfirst.org/violation-tracker/wv-crown-cork-and-seal-company-inc), [General Court Cans Crown and Silgan Appeals Against The Commission’s Metal Packaging Cartel Decision | Cleary Antitrust Watch](https://www.clearyantitrustwatch.com/2024/10/general-court-cans-crown-and-silgan-appeals-against-the-commissions-metal-packaging-cartel-decision/). Crown opted for a settlement procedure, admitting involvement in exchange for a 10% fine reduction [Antitrust: Commission fines the metal packaging producers Crown and Silgan €31.5 million in cartel settlement](https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_22_4483). The EU General Court dismissed Crown's subsequent appeal on October 2, 2024 [General Court Cans Crown and Silgan Appeals Against The Commission’s Metal Packaging Cartel Decision | Cleary Antitrust Watch](https://www.clearyantitrustwatch.com/2024/10/general-court-cans-crown-and-silgan-appeals-against-the-commissions-metal-packaging-cartel-decision/).
*   **DOJ Customs Lawsuit:** In July 2021, the U.S. Department of Justice filed a lawsuit (Case 1:21-cv-00361) in the U.S. Court of International Trade against Crown Cork & Seal subsidiaries, seeking up to $18.1 million in penalties [Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/international-trade/crown-holdings-faces-18-million-lawsuit-over-metal-lid-imports). The DOJ alleged the company fraudulently misclassified 543 entries of metal lid imports between 2004 and 2009 to avoid over $1.1 million in duties [IN THE UNITED STATES COURT OF INTERNATIONAL TRADE BEFORE: THE UNITED STATES, )](https://millerco.com/sites/default/files/2021-08/US%20v.%20Crown%20Cork%20Seal%20CIT%20Complaint%20(00222295xB740D).PDF). The court issued an opinion in 2023, though the final settlement status is unconfirmed [United States v. Crown Cork & Seal USA, Inc., No. 21-00361 (Ct. Int'l Trade 2023) :: Justia](https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/appellate-courts/cit/21-00361/21-00361-2023-02-27.html).
*   **SEC Settlement:** In July 2022, Crown Holdings reached a settlement with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) relating to an investigation, though specific allegations were not disclosed [UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION WASHINGTON, DC 20549 FORM 10-Q](https://crowncork.gcs-web.com/static-files/5c34d37b-9bcd-4a7b-b5bc-31c628e54158).

### Sanctions and Export-Control Exposure
*   **Sanctions:** There is no evidence in the provided findings that Crown Holdings, Inc. or its subsidiaries are currently designated under OFAC sanctions. *(Note: OFAC penalties against "King Holdings LLC", "Toll Holdings", and "Crown Holdings LLC" involve entirely distinct entities [OFAC Imposes $4.7 Million Penalty on Real Estate Investor for Dealing in Blocked Russian’s Property | U.S. Department of the Treasury](https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0323), [Crown Holdings - Crown Holdings](https://crown.holdings/)).*
*   **Russia Operations:** Crown Holdings' current operational status in Russia is contested. The company historically operated a packaging line in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, in partnership with Bonduelle [Bonduelle and Crown Partner In Russia | Crown](https://www.crowncork.com/news/bonduelle-and-crown-partner-russia). Crown divested its European Tinplate business (Eviosys) between 2021 and December 2024 [Crown Holdings, Inc. to Receive Proceeds From Closing of Eviosys Sale to Sonoco Products Company | Crown](https://www.crowncork.com/news/crown-holdings-inc-receive-proceeds-closing-eviosys-sale-sonoco-products-company). The company's 2024 Annual Report does not list any facilities in Russia under its European Beverage segment [A N N U A L R E P O R T 2 0 2 4 BUILT TO PERFORM](https://www.crowncork.com/sites/default/files/2025-03/2024_Annual_Report-Crown_Holdings_Inc.pdf). However, the monitoring project 'LeaveRussia' categorized Crown Holdings as "Staying" and "Doing Business in Russia as Usual" as of June 2022 [LeaveRussia: Crown Holdings is Doing Business in Russia as Usual](https://leave-russia.org/crown-holdings). 

### Product Recalls and Quality Issues
*   **Asbestos Product Liability:** Crown Holdings faces significant legacy product liability litigation stemming from its 1963 acquisition of Mundet Cork, an asbestos insulation manufacturer [Crown Cork & Seal Co. | Asbestos Products & Lawsuits](https://www.mesothelioma.com/asbestos-exposure/companies/crown-cork-seal-company/). As of 2022, the company estimated its current and future asbestos-related liabilities at approximately $220 million [Crown Cork & Seal Co. | Asbestos Products & Lawsuits](https://www.mesothelioma.com/asbestos-exposure/companies/crown-cork-seal-company/). Notable past losses include a 2012 Louisiana jury verdict of $12 million (Crown found 33% responsible) and a $5.1 million award to the family of William Saller [Crown Cork and Seal Lawsuits and Former Insurance Fund](https://www.asbestos.com/companies/crown-cork-and-seal/), [Crown Cork & Seal | Asbestos Exposure and Lawsuits](https://www.mesotheliomalawyercenter.org/asbestos/manufacturers/crown-cork-seal/).

### ESG, Environmental, and Labor Controversies
*   **Environmental Fines:** Crown Cork & Seal Company agreed to pay $1,945,305 to the Olympic Region Clean Air Agency (ORCAA) to settle nine air quality violations occurring between 2021 and 2023 at its Olympia, Washington facility [Crown Cork & Seal agrees to fines for repeated air quality violations | The JOLT News Organization](https://www.thejoltnews.com/stories/crown-cork-seal-agrees-to-fines-for-repeated-air-quality-violations,13865). Violations included bypassing emission control equipment, exceeding permissible formaldehyde limits, and beginning construction without permits [Crown Cork & Seal agrees to fines for repeated air quality violations | The JOLT News Organization](https://www.thejoltnews.com/stories/crown-cork-seal-agrees-to-fines-for-repeated-air-quality-violations,13865). Violation Tracker reports the company has accumulated $10,706,380 in total penalties from 69 records since 2000 [crown-holdings-inc | Violation Tracker](https://violationtracker.goodjobsfirst.org/parent/crown-holdings-inc).
*   **Labor Disputes:** The company has a history of contentious labor relations. In September 2013, 120 United Steelworkers members at a Toronto factory initiated a strike lasting at least 17 months over demanded 42% pay cuts for new employees and reduced pensions, which sparked a consumer boycott in Canada [Crown boycott reaches US settlement](https://www.beveragedaily.com/Article/2015/04/20/Crown-boycott-reaches-US-settlement/), [Global fight for workers’ rights continues at Crown Holdings](https://www.industriall-union.org/global-fight-for-workers-rights-continues-at-crown-holdings). The company also faced allegations in Turkey of dismissing union activists and refusing to negotiate with officially recognized unions [Global fight for workers’ rights continues at Crown Holdings](https://www.industriall-union.org/global-fight-for-workers-rights-continues-at-crown-holdings).

---

## Gaps and Limitations
Based on the provided research findings, the following information could not be verified:
1.  **DOJ Lawsuit Resolution:** The final settlement amount or current docket status of the 2021 DOJ customs lawsuit ($18.1 million) is unknown beyond a 2023 court opinion.
2.  **SEC Settlement Details:** The specific allegations and financial penalties associated with the July 2022 SEC settlement were not found.
3.  **Recent Product Recalls:** No specific FDA warnings, product recalls, or breach of warranty claims from the last 5 years were identified in the provided data.
4.  **Russian Operations:** Definitive confirmation of whether Crown Holdings completely ceased all operations in Russia post-2022 could not be established due to conflicting reports between the company's 2024 Annual Report and third-party monitors.

sources.name: Crown boycott reaches US settlement

sources.name: Crown boycott reaches US settlement

sources.name: Global fight for workers’ rights continues at Crown Holdings

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal Co. | Asbestos Products & Lawsuits
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings holds significant asbestos liabilities stemming from its 1963 acquisition of Mundet Cork, a company that manufactured asbestos-containing insulation.

sources.name: Crown Cork and Seal Lawsuits and Former Insurance Fund

sources.name: Crown Holdings, Inc. to Receive Proceeds From Closing of Eviosys Sale to Sonoco Products Company | Crown
sources.snippet: On December 4, 2024, Crown Holdings completed the sale of its remaining 20% equity stake in Eviosys (the former European Tinplate business) to Sonoco Products Company for approximately $300 million.

sources.name: Crown Holdings, Inc. to Receive Proceeds From Closing of Eviosys Sale to Sonoco Products Company | Crown
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings' Chief Financial Officer, Kevin Clothier, stated on December 4, 2024, that the sale of the remaining stake in Eviosys 'completes the strategic divestiture of our European Tinplate business.'

sources.name: Bonduelle and Crown Partner In Russia | Crown
sources.snippet: In 2013, Crown Food Europe partnered with the Bonduelle Group to install a metal packaging production line inside Bonduelle's Novotitarovskaia plant in Krasnodar Krai, southern Russia.

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports

sources.name: UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION WASHINGTON, DC 20549 FORM 10-Q

sources.name: UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION WASHINGTON, DC 20549 FORM 10-Q
sources.snippet: In July 2022, Crown Holdings reached a settlement with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) relating to an investigation by the Commission.

sources.name: General Court Cans Crown and Silgan Appeals Against The Commission’s Metal Packaging Cartel Decision | Cleary Antitrust Watch
sources.snippet: On July 12, 2022, the European Commission fined Crown and Silgan a total of €31.5 million for participating in a metal packaging cartel in Germany between 2011 and 2014.

sources.name: General Court Cans Crown and Silgan Appeals Against The Commission’s Metal Packaging Cartel Decision | Cleary Antitrust Watch
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings opted for a settlement procedure regarding the European Commission's cartel decision, which included a 10% fine reduction in exchange for not contesting the legal and factual assessment.

sources.name: Bonduelle and Crown Partner In Russia | Crown
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings, through its Crown Food Europe business unit, historically partnered with the Bonduelle Group to install a metal packaging production line at the Novotitarovskaia plant in Krasnodar Krai, Russia.

sources.name: A N N U A L R E P O R T 2 0 2 4 BUILT TO PERFORM
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings' 2024 Annual Report highlights its 'North American Tinplate' business as a core segment, but does not list a corresponding European Tinplate or European Food segment in its primary business strength summary, following the company's historical exit from certain packaging sectors.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal agrees to fines for repeated air quality violations | The JOLT News Organization
sources.snippet: Crown Cork & Seal Company, Inc. (a subsidiary of Crown Holdings) agreed to pay a financial penalty of $1,945,305 to the Olympic Region Clean Air Agency (ORCAA) to settle air quality violations.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal agrees to fines for repeated air quality violations | The JOLT News Organization
sources.snippet: The ORCAA penalty resulted from nine air quality violations occurring between 2021 and 2023, including the expansion of a facility before securing necessary permits.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal agrees to fines for repeated air quality violations | The JOLT News Organization
sources.snippet: Individual fines for the ORCAA air quality violations ranged from $18,000 to $744,000.

sources.name: UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION WASHINGTON, DC 20549 FORM 10-Q
sources.snippet: Crown Holdings' Bowling Green plant resumed operations in March 2022 after being curtailed due to tornado damage sustained in December 2021.

sources.name: UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION WASHINGTON, DC 20549 FORM 10-Q
sources.snippet: On July 28, 2022, the Crown Holdings Board of Directors declared a dividend of $0.22 per share payable on August 25, 2022.

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports
sources.snippet: The U.S. Department of Justice filed a lawsuit (Case 1:21-cv-00361) against Crown Cork & Seal USA, Inc. in the U.S. Court of International Trade.

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports
sources.snippet: The lawsuit alleged that Crown Cork & Seal fraudulently misclassified imports of metal can lids to avoid paying more than $1.1 million in duties and fees.

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports
sources.snippet: The Department of Justice sought a maximum penalty of $18.1 million against Crown Holdings Inc. in the U.S. Court of International Trade.

sources.name: United States v. Crown Cork & Seal USA, Inc., No. 21-00361 (Ct. Int'l Trade 2023) :: Justia
sources.snippet: The U.S. Court of International Trade issued a case opinion for Case No. 21-00361 in 2023.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal Co. | Asbestos Products & Lawsuits
sources.snippet: As of 2022, Crown Cork & Seal estimated its current and future asbestos-related liabilities to be approximately $220 million.

sources.name: Crown Holdings Faces $18 Million Lawsuit Over Metal Lid Imports
sources.snippet: The U.S. Justice Department filed a lawsuit against Crown Holdings Inc. seeking up to $18.1 million in fines for allegedly misclassifying imports of metal can lids to avoid more than $1.1 million in duties and fees.

sources.name: Crown Cork and Seal Lawsuits and Former Insurance Fund
sources.snippet: A Louisiana jury in June 2012 found Crown 33% responsible for a $12 million verdict in a mesothelioma lawsuit filed by Frederick Schulte.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal Co. | Asbestos Products & Lawsuits
sources.snippet: In 2013, a California jury awarded nearly $1.5 million in a wrongful death lawsuit to the family of an oil refinery worker who died from mesothelioma linked to Mundet insulation products.

sources.name: Crown Cork & Seal | Asbestos Exposure and Lawsuits
sources.snippet: The family of William Saller won a $5.1 million award, including compensatory and punitive damages, in a lawsuit against Crown Cork & Seal related to asbestos exposure.
